howItWorks.intro: This filament length calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How many meters of filament are on a 1kg spool?
faq.answer: A 1kg spool of 1.75mm PLA contains approximately 335 meters. ABS has about 400m and PETG about 370m per kg due to different densities.

faq.question: Why does material type affect filament length?
faq.answer: Different materials have different densities. Lighter materials (like ABS at 1.04 g/cm³) yield more length per kilogram than denser materials (like PLA at 1.24 g/cm³).

faq.question: Does 1.75mm vs 2.85mm filament affect length?
faq.answer: Yes, significantly. 2.85mm filament is about 2.65x thicker in cross-section, so a 1kg spool contains roughly 1/2.65 the length of 1.75mm filament.

faq.question: Is the Filament Length Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard 3D printing and additive manufacturing formulas trusted by makers and 3D printing enthusiasts. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Filament Length Calculator?
sections.body: The Filament Length Calculator is a free online tool designed for makers and 3D printing enthusiasts who need quick, accurate calculations in the 3D printing and additive manufacturing space. By entering your filament weight, filament diameter, material density, you get instant results including filament length, filament length, cost per meter. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting filament length right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In 3D printing and additive manufacturing, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by makers and 3D printing enthusiasts worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to optimize your prints with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your filament weight and need to find the right filament length. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.description: Type or select your filament diameter in the field provided. Use the most accurate value available for best results.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results

tips: Double-check your filament weight before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
